text.title: Load Balancer Health Checks Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for sound health-check design. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Liveness and readiness MUST be distinct endpoints. Liveness (`/health/live`) returns 200 if the process can serve any request; failure triggers Kubernetes pod restart. Readiness (`/health/ready`) returns 200 only when ALL critical dependencies respond within budget; failure removes the pod from the LB pool WITHOUT restart.
rule.rationale: Using the same endpoint for both probes creates restart loops — a slow dependency (DB migration mid-startup) triggers liveness failure and kills the pod before it can ever serve traffic.

rule.statement: The readiness probe MUST actively check every external dependency the request path needs: primary DB (`SELECT 1`), cache (`PING`), broker (`AMQP heartbeat` or `Kafka admin`). A probe that returns 200 without touching dependencies is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Shallow probes mask backend failure — the LB happily routes traffic to a backend that cannot serve it. Deep probes return 503 the moment a dependency dies; the LB removes the backend within one check interval.

rule.statement: Every dependency call inside `/health/ready` MUST be wrapped in a 1–5 s timeout (e.g., `context.WithTimeout` in Go, `psycopg2.connect(connect_timeout=5)` in Python, `redis.ping().timeout(5)` in Node). The probe handler MUST return 503 when any timeout fires.
rule.rationale: Without a timeout, a hung dependency blocks the probe forever; the LB declares the backend unhealthy from the LB's own timeout instead of the backend's deliberate 503, costing diagnosability.

rule.statement: Check interval MUST be 10–30 s. Healthy threshold ≥ 2 consecutive successes (cloud LB) or ≥ 2 (HAProxy `rise 2`). Unhealthy threshold ≥ 2 (cloud) or ≥ 3 (HAProxy `fall 3`). 1-second intervals or 1-failure thresholds are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sub-10 s intervals create probe storms — at 1000 backends × 1 s, the LB spends meaningful CPU just probing. Single-failure thresholds flap during transient GC pauses.

rule.statement: LB-side check config MUST assert response status AND, where applicable, response-body match. HAProxy: `http-check expect status 200`. Nginx: `health_check status_match=200`. Kubernetes httpGet: `success_threshold` ≥ 2.
rule.rationale: Without `expect`, any response (502 from a still-listening proxy in front of a crashed app) counts as healthy.
